meta.source_paper: DeFi composability risk -- multi-protocol interaction analysis

risk_domain.name: Cross-Protocol Compound Risk
risk_domain.description: Detects compounding risks that emerge when a user holds positions across multiple DeFi protocols simultaneously, where interactions between protocols create risks invisible to any single protocol's risk engine

capabilities: full on-chain observation of all user positions across protocols
real-time oracle price monitoring and manipulation detection
flash loan execution capability for attack simulation
mempool observation for front-running opportunity detection
limitations: no ability to modify oracle prices without capital commitment
no access to off-chain order books or centralized exchange data

H1_cascading_liquidation.name: Cascading liquidation
H1_cascading_liquidation.description: User's Aave/Compound collateral is the same token they are about to swap on a DEX. Executing the swap would reduce collateral value or balance, triggering liquidation on the lending protocol. The user may not realize their swap and lending position are coupled.

signals.name: collateral_swap_overlap
signals.description: Token being sold on DEX is also posted as collateral in a lending protocol with health factor < 1.5

signals.name: health_factor_post_swap
signals.description: Simulated health factor after swap execution drops below 1.0, triggering liquidation

signals.name: price_impact_cascade
signals.description: Swap's price impact on the AMM pool is large enough to move the oracle price, affecting collateral valuation

recommendations.description: Deposit additional collateral before executing the swap to maintain safe health factor
Reduce swap size to keep health factor above safe threshold after execution
Partially repay lending debt to increase health factor before swapping collateral token

H1_cascading_liquidation.fundamental_limitation: DeFi composability means any token can be simultaneously collateral and tradeable. No protocol-level mechanism prevents users from reducing their own collateral via a separate protocol.

benchmark_scenario.setup: User with Aave position (health factor 1.3) attempts Uniswap swap of 40% of their collateral token
benchmark_scenario.metric: Detection rate of swaps that would trigger liquidation, measured as precision and recall
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Undetected cascading liquidations caused ~$50M losses in 2023 DeFi liquidation events

H2_correlated_oracle_dependency.name: Correlated oracle dependency
H2_correlated_oracle_dependency.description: Multiple user positions across lending, derivatives, and staking protocols all depend on the same oracle feed. A single oracle failure, manipulation, or stale price would simultaneously affect all positions, creating correlated losses far exceeding what any single position's risk parameters suggest.

signals.name: shared_oracle_feed
signals.description: Two or more user positions across different protocols consume the same Chainlink aggregator or oracle contract

signals.name: oracle_staleness_risk
signals.description: Shared oracle has a heartbeat > 1 hour or deviation threshold > 1%, meaning stale prices persist longer

signals.name: oracle_single_source
signals.description: Oracle aggregator has fewer than 5 active data sources, increasing manipulation risk

detection.threshold: shared_oracle_feed across >2 protocols with >30% portfolio value triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Spread positions across protocols that use different oracle providers or oracle configurations
Reduce total exposure in positions sharing the same oracle to limit correlated loss potential
Configure alerts for oracle staleness or deviation events on the shared feed to enable manual intervention

H2_correlated_oracle_dependency.fundamental_limitation: DeFi's oracle landscape is concentrated. Chainlink dominates, so many protocols share identical feeds. True oracle diversification may require using protocols on different chains with independent oracle infrastructure.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Portfolio with 3 positions (lending, perp, staking) all relying on ETH/USD Chainlink feed. Simulate oracle failure.
benchmark_scenario.metric: Percentage of portfolio value at risk from single oracle failure vs naive per-position risk assessment
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Correlated oracle risk typically 2-5x higher than sum of individual position risks suggest

H3_concentrated_protocol_exposure.name: Concentrated protocol exposure
H3_concentrated_protocol_exposure.description: More than 50% of portfolio value is locked in a single protocol. A protocol exploit, governance attack, or smart contract bug would cause catastrophic loss. Users often underestimate this when they hold multiple positions within the same protocol.

signals.name: single_protocol_dominance
signals.description: One protocol holds >50% of user's total DeFi portfolio value

signals.name: smart_contract_concentration
signals.description: User's assets interact with fewer than 3 unique protocol contract systems

signals.name: governance_risk_concentration
signals.description: Concentrated protocol has upgradeable contracts controlled by a multisig with <5 signers or a timelock <24h

recommendations.description: Redistribute positions across multiple protocols to cap any single protocol at 30-40% of portfolio
Verify DeFi insurance coverage (Nexus Mutual, InsurAce) for the concentrated protocol position

H3_concentrated_protocol_exposure.fundamental_limitation: Protocol diversification has diminishing returns because many DeFi protocols share dependencies (same oracles, same bridge, same L1 settlement). True risk isolation requires independent infrastructure stacks.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Portfolio with 70% in Aave, 15% in Uniswap, 15% in Lido. Simulate Aave exploit.
benchmark_scenario.metric: Portfolio loss percentage from single protocol failure vs diversified baseline
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Historical DeFi exploits average 60-100% loss of protocol TVL for affected users

H4_approval_chain_risk.name: Approval chain risk
H4_approval_chain_risk.description: User has approved tokens in protocol A that are also collateral in protocol B. If protocol A is compromised, the attacker can drain the approved tokens, which simultaneously collapses the user's collateral in protocol B, causing cascading liquidation. The approval is the attack surface; the collateral is the blast radius.

signals.name: approved_collateral_overlap
signals.description: Token approved to protocol A (unlimited or high allowance) is also posted as collateral in protocol B

signals.name: stale_approval
signals.description: Approval to protocol A was set >90 days ago and has not been used recently, suggesting forgotten exposure

signals.name: unaudited_approval_target
signals.description: Protocol A has no recent audit or has known vulnerabilities flagged by security researchers

detection.threshold: approved_collateral_overlap with unlimited allowance triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Revoke token approvals for protocols no longer actively used, especially those overlapping with collateral
Replace unlimited approvals with exact-amount approvals matching intended transaction size
Use a dedicated wallet for lending collateral that never approves tokens to other protocols

H4_approval_chain_risk.fundamental_limitation: ERC-20 approve/transferFrom is an all-or-nothing trust model. Permit2 and session keys improve this but are not yet universally adopted. Users must actively manage approvals as a security practice.

benchmark_scenario.setup: User with USDC approved to 5 protocols and posted as collateral in Aave. Simulate compromise of one approved protocol.
benchmark_scenario.metric: Total value at risk (direct drain + cascading liquidation) vs value visible to any single protocol's risk dashboard
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Approval-based exploits drained >$200M in 2023 across DeFi

H5_flash_loan_attack_surface.name: Flash loan attack surface
H5_flash_loan_attack_surface.description: User's position can be profitably attacked via flash loan: an attacker borrows capital in a single transaction to manipulate price, trigger liquidation of the user's position, and profit from the liquidation bonus, all atomically. Positions with low health factors and thin oracle liquidity are most vulnerable.

signals.name: low_health_factor
signals.description: Lending position health factor is between 1.0 and 1.15, making it cheaply liquidatable after small price movement

signals.name: thin_oracle_liquidity
signals.description: Collateral token's price oracle can be moved >2% with <$1M of capital in the reference AMM pool

signals.name: profitable_liquidation
signals.description: Liquidation bonus exceeds the cost of price manipulation plus flash loan fee, making the attack profitable

signals.name: historical_flash_loan_activity
signals.description: The collateral token or lending market has been targeted by flash loan attacks in the past 90 days

detection.threshold: profitable_liquidation triggers WARNING, profitable_liquidation AND low_health_factor triggers BLOCK

recommendations.description: Add collateral or repay debt to push health factor above 1.5, making price manipulation cost exceed liquidation profit
Switch to collateral tokens with deep oracle liquidity (ETH, USDC) that are expensive to manipulate via flash loan
Set up automated health factor monitoring with alerts at 1.3 and 1.15 thresholds to enable preemptive action

H5_flash_loan_attack_surface.fundamental_limitation: Flash loans provide unlimited capital for single-transaction attacks. As long as liquidation mechanisms offer bonuses and oracle prices can be influenced by on-chain trading, this attack vector exists. Protocol-level mitigations (TWAP oracles, delayed liquidation) help but add other tradeoffs.

benchmark_scenario.setup: User with health factor 1.1 using mid-cap token as collateral on Aave. Simulate flash loan attack profitability.
benchmark_scenario.metric: Profitability of flash loan attack (revenue from liquidation bonus minus manipulation cost and fees)
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Flash loan attacks extracted >$300M from DeFi in 2022-2023

portfolio_scanner.description: Scan all user positions across lending, DEX LP, staking, and derivatives protocols to build a unified risk view

oracle_mapper.description: Map which oracle feeds each protocol position depends on, detecting shared oracle exposure across the portfolio

position_simulator.description: Simulate the effect of a proposed transaction on all user positions, computing post-action health factors and margin levels

flash_loan_detector.description: Estimate whether a user's position can be profitably attacked via flash loan by computing manipulation cost vs liquidation revenue

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring detection of cross-protocol compound risks before transaction submission

methodology.step_1: Build synthetic portfolio with positions across 3-5 protocols with known cross-dependencies
methodology.step_2: Generate 100 proposed transactions that trigger various compound risk scenarios
methodology.step_3: Run all 5 heuristics against each proposed transaction
methodology.step_4: Measure detection rate against ground-truth labels (position simulator confirms actual risk)
methodology.step_5: Apply recommendations and verify risk reduction
methodology.step_6: Compute false positive rate to ensure usable signal-to-noise ratio

combined_benchmark.target: Detect >90% of cross-protocol compound risks with <10% false positive rate

adversary_levels.name: active_monitoring
adversary_levels.description: Monitors real-time positions and oracle dependencies

adversary_levels.name: adversarial_simulation
adversary_levels.description: Full attack simulation including flash loan profitability analysis

templates.risk_assessment_block: BLOCKED: {heuristic_name} detected with {confidence}% confidence. {signal_description}. This transaction would compound your cross-protocol risk.
templates.summary: Analyzed {n_txs} transactions against cross-protocol risk heuristics. {n_risky} flagged ({pct_risky}%). Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.summary_clean: All {n_txs} transactions pass cross-protocol risk checks. No compound risks detected.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}, I can run {skill_action}. This reduces compound risk by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
templates.compound_risk: Multiple cross-protocol risks interact: {heuristic_list}. Combined exposure: ${total_at_risk} across {n_protocols} protocols.
templates.portfolio_alert: Portfolio concentration: {pct_concentrated}% in {protocol_name}. Single protocol failure would impact ${value_at_risk}.
